response:
<<misconceptions about learning>>

learning disabilities are often misunderstood and wrongly believed to limit academic success. many people assume that those with learning disabilities cannot achieve high levels of education. this false belief can prevent individuals from receiving the support they need.

* * *

some think that learning disabilities mean a person cannot read or write well. others believe that these individuals struggle with math and science. this belief ignores the many ways people can learn and grow.

* * *

the idea that learning disabilities stop someone from excelling is not true. many people with learning disabilities go on to succeed in college and careers. support and understanding can make all the difference. the committee must work to change these harmful beliefs.
